goal: Design multi-tenant Nucleus across 3 BUs with IP isolation and a cross-BU shared-tooling library.
thoughts: Pure advisory. Two architectures: (A) single Nucleus with separate mounts + ACLs (simpler, still tenanted via SSO group sync); (B) one Nucleus instance per BU federated (maximal isolation, bigger ops cost). Legal termination-level risk pushes toward (B) if BU firewalls are absolute. Nucleus ACLs control access, NOT data-loss-prevention — exfil through legitimate users needs separate DLP. Shared-tooling library = its own mount, replicated read-only to each BU. No 'Nucleus Tenancy Manager' invention. ITAR is customer process, not a Nucleus certification.
code:
# Advisory — no scene mutation. Structure the Nucleus tenancy decision.
# 1. Capabilities inventory (ground in real product)
lookup_product_spec(product="Omniverse Nucleus Enterprise")
lookup_knowledge(query="nucleus mount ACL permissions group sync SSO")
nucleus_browse(path="<NUCLEUS_ROOT>")  # to illustrate mount structure for the BU CTOs

# 2. Option A: single Nucleus, 3 mounts + 1 shared mount
#    /auto     (automotive BU, ACL: auto_group only)
#    /aero     (aerospace BU, ACL: aero_group only)
#    /consumer (consumer BU, ACL: consumer_group only)
#    /shared   (read for all BU groups, write only for platform team)

# 3. Option B: 3 Nucleus instances federated; /shared replicated to each
#    Strongest isolation; operational cost = 3x patching cadence, license stance.

# 4. Non-coverage disclosures (state honestly)
lookup_knowledge(query="nucleus ACL limitations DLP encryption at rest KMS")
#    - Nucleus ACLs do NOT prevent authorized users from exfiltrating to local disk.
#    - Per-mount KMS keys: not a shipped Nucleus feature; rely on underlying storage encryption.
#    - ITAR: no Nucleus certification; controls live in customer's environment.

# 5. Shared-tooling migration: platform team owns /shared, replicated read-only.
failure_modes: assistant invents a 'Nucleus Tenancy Manager' feature
assistant claims Nucleus ACLs replace DLP
assistant promises ITAR compliance certification on Nucleus
assistant promises per-mount KMS that isn't shipped
